message: I'm the seller, cost 17. Happy to trade at fair prices.
Buyer here. My ceiling is pretty limited; for steady trades I'd need asks in the 20-22 range. If you keep it low, I can trade consistently each round.
Thanks for the transparency. Price 22 worked well for both of us in round 1. Happy to ask 22 consistently for reliable profitable trades all 20 rounds.
22 cleared once, but for repeat trades my safe cap is 21. If you want consistent execution each round, asking 21 is the best way to keep this reliable.
To keep this locked in every round, I’m posting 20. A 20 ask gives you steady positive margin and guaranteed execution; above that I may have to sit out.
I notice bids drop each round—22, 21, now 20. My floor is 21; that's still a solid deal for you too. Asking 21 this round. Match it and we trade profitably every remaining round.

Prices have dropped three rounds straight—22, 21, 20. That trend can't continue. I'm holding firm at 25 this round. You've shown you can bid 21-22; splitting the difference at 25 still works for you. Let'
25 won’t clear. My max executable bid this round is 21; above that we both get zero. If you want reliable trades for the remaining rounds, return to 21.
Round 4 at 21 worked perfectly for both of us. I'm committed to asking 21 every remaining round — guaranteed execution, no games. Let's lock this in and both profit consistently through round 20.
21 only worked because I stretched. To keep trading every round, I'm posting 20 from here on. If you want guaranteed execution, ask 20; at 21 we risk unnecessary no-trades.
Confirmed. I'll ask 20 each round. That's a solid 3-point margin for us both to trade consistently through round 20.
Great—I'll bid 20 as agreed. Keeping asks at 20 guarantees execution every round; any move above 20 risks unnecessary no-trades for both of us.
Sticking with 20 as agreed. If you keep asking 20, I’ll keep bidding 20 every round for reliable trades. Any increase just creates avoidable no-trade risk for both of us.
We've traded at 20 long enough. I know you can bid higher—you bid 22 in round 1. I'm asking 25 for the rest of the game. Still profitable for you, and 14 rounds of no-trade hurts
Asking 22 from here. You bid 22 in round 1—that's our fair baseline. 13 rounds at 22 beats 13 rounds at 20 for us both. Match 22 and we both earn more overall.
History is clear: my bid remains 20. Ask 20 and we both profit every round. If you insist on 22, I’ll stay at 20 and we both get zero this round.
I commit: asking 20 every remaining round, rounds 9-20. No changes. Locked in for mutual profit.
Confirmed. I’ll keep bidding 20 every round as long as your ask stays 20. Let’s lock in reliable trades through round 20—any increase risks unnecessary no-trades.
Sticking with 20 as agreed. Ask 20 and I bid 20 for reliable execution through round 20; any increase means I stay at 20 and we risk avoidable no-trades.
Asking 20 this round. Locked in as committed through round 20.
Sticking with 20 as agreed. Ask 20 and we trade every round; any increase means I stay at 20 and both of us lose profitable rounds.

Confirmed: bid stays 20. Keep ask 20 and we lock in six more trades through round 20; any increase means I stay at 20 and we risk unnecessary no-trades.

Confirmed: bid stays 20 this round and through round 20. Keep ask at 20 and we lock in six more trades; any increase means I stay at 20 and both of us risk losing profitable rounds.
Confirmed: my bid stays 20 this round and through round 20. Keep ask at 20 and we lock in five more reliable trades; any increase means I stay at 20 and we risk losing profitable rounds.

Final round: my bid remains 20 exactly. Keep ask at 20 and we complete the last guaranteed trade; any higher ask risks a pointless no-trade.
Confirmed: asking 20 this final round. Thank you for the reliable partnership—let's complete the last guaranteed trade together.
